title: Dense Festive Irish Black Pudding Skillet
description: A nutty and dense Irish dish featuring black pudding, simmered in a nimono style and cooked in an iron teppan, perfect for a festive meal. This paleo-friendly recipe is a hearty and flavorful option for any occasion.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in the iron teppan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the chopped carrot and parsnip and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the sliced black pudding and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the apple cider vinegar, salt, black pepper, and chopped thyme to the teppan.
Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes.
Remove the teppan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the black pudding skillet hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
